metadata.archetype_justification: This is a CLI tool (npm package) with no persistent data store, no deployed workload of its own, no API surface, and no long-running processes. All operations are stateless command-line invocations that read config files and make AWS SDK calls.
metadata.context: Node.js CLI for deploying AWS Lambda functions.

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration exists
top_gaps.impact: No visibility into CLI actions or deployment audit trail

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: No authentication layer; credentials exposed via CLI arguments
top_gaps.impact: Credentials exposed in shell history and process lists

top_gaps.title: Centralized Identity Integration
top_gaps.gap: No centralized IdP integration; no SSO support
top_gaps.impact: Users manage credentials manually with no federated identity

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: X-Ray SDK imported but only for local Lambda simulation
top_gaps.impact: No observability into CLI deployment operations

top_gaps.title: API Versioning Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No versioning strategy for CLI commands or flag contracts
top_gaps.impact: Breaking changes deployed without migration path

findings.title: AWS SDK v2 in maintenance mode
findings.description: JavaScript (CommonJS) on Node.js >= 22.0.0 with AWS SDK v2. The language runtime is modern but the AWS SDK is outdated (v2 in maintenance mode). continuation-local-storage is also deprecated.
findings.gap: AWS SDK v2 is in maintenance mode. Should migrate to modular AWS SDK v3. continuation-local-storage should be replaced with native AsyncLocalStorage.
findings.recommendation: Migrate from aws-sdk v2 to @aws-sdk/client-lambda, @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch-logs, @aws-sdk/client-cloudwatch-events. Replace continuation-local-storage with native AsyncLocalStorage.

findings.title: Monolithic CLI with shared mutable state
findings.description: Single deployable unit with identifiable modules but shared mutable AWS SDK singleton. Modules have clear responsibilities but direct coupling through shared state.
findings.gap: Shared mutable state via aws.sdk singleton. lib/aws.js mutates global aws.config affecting all modules. No clear interfaces between modules.
findings.recommendation: Inject AWS client instances rather than sharing a mutable global singleton. This improves testability and future modularization.

findings.title: No CLI versioning strategy
findings.description: No API versioning strategy for CLI commands or flags. Breaking changes have no migration path beyond CHANGELOG notes.
findings.gap: No versioning strategy for CLI commands or flag contracts. Users have no guaranteed migration path when flags change.
findings.recommendation: Implement a deprecation policy for CLI flags with warnings before removal. Adhere strictly to semver where major versions signal breaking flag changes.

findings.title: Environment-variable-based endpoint configuration
findings.description: AWS endpoints configured via environment variables and CLI flags. No hard-coded endpoints but no dynamic service discovery. Acceptable for CLI tool.
findings.gap: Endpoint configuration entirely through environment variables. For a CLI tool this is acceptable but not dynamic discovery.
findings.recommendation: Current approach is appropriate for a CLI tool. No change needed.

findings.title: No audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or audit logging configuration. CLI uses only console.log for status output with no structured audit trail of deployment actions.
findings.gap: No audit logging of CLI deployment operations. No local record of what was deployed, when, and with what parameters.
findings.recommendation: Add structured logging of deployment operations to a local log file. Consider --audit-log option.

findings.title: Credentials exposed via CLI arguments
findings.description: No authentication layer. AWS credentials accepted via CLI flags (--accessKey, --secretKey) exposing them in shell history and process lists.
findings.gap: Credentials passed as CLI arguments are exposed in shell history and process lists. Direct credential flags are a security concern.
findings.recommendation: Deprecate --accessKey and --secretKey CLI flags in favor of AWS profile-based authentication or SSO. Add documentation warning about credential exposure.

findings.title: No centralized identity integration
findings.description: No centralized IdP integration. Only supports SharedIniFileCredentials (profile) or explicit access key/secret. No AWS SSO or federation support.
findings.gap: No SSO or federated identity support. Users managing multiple AWS accounts have no streamlined auth flow.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) credential resolution by leveraging AWS SDK v3 built-in credential provider chain.

findings.title: Credentials managed via env vars without rotation
findings.description: No plaintext credentials in source code. Credentials read from environment variables or CLI arguments at runtime. No integration with Secrets Manager or rotation mechanism.
findings.gap: No secrets management integration. Recommended workflow stores credentials in .env files (plaintext on filesystem) without rotation.
findings.recommendation: Add documentation recommending AWS IAM Identity Center (SSO) over .env file storage. SDK v3 migration would enable full credential provider chain.

findings.title: No vulnerability scanning in CI
findings.description: Dependabot configured for monthly updates. No npm audit or runtime vulnerability scanning in CI pipeline. CodeQL covers SAST only.
findings.gap: No npm audit step in CI. Dependabot provides reactive updates but does not block vulnerable releases.
findings.recommendation: Add npm audit --audit-level=high to CI pipeline as a blocking step.

findings.title: CodeQL SAST without dependency scanning gate
findings.description: CodeQL scanning integrated in CI on push/PR and weekly schedule. Dependabot configured. No npm audit step or blocking gate on findings.
findings.gap: No explicit dependency vulnerability scanning in CI. No blocking gate on security findings.
findings.recommendation: Add npm audit --audit-level=high as blocking CI step to complement CodeQL SAST.

findings.title: No distributed tracing for CLI operations
findings.description: aws-xray-sdk-core imported but only used for local Lambda simulation context. Does not instrument the CLI tool itself.
findings.gap: No distributed tracing of CLI deployment operations. X-Ray import is solely for Lambda local simulation support.
findings.recommendation: For a CLI tool, distributed tracing has limited value. Low priority. Consider OpenTelemetry if deployment monitoring is needed.

findings.title: No business or usage metrics
findings.description: No custom metrics published. CLI outputs to console.log only. No telemetry, deployment tracking, or usage metrics.
findings.gap: No metrics on deployment success rates, package sizes, deployment durations, or error rates.
findings.recommendation: Consider opt-in anonymous telemetry for deployment metrics. Low priority for open-source CLI.

findings.title: No anomaly detection or alerting
findings.description: No anomaly detection or alerting configured. CLI tool with no runtime service to monitor.
findings.gap: No alerting on package health or CI failure patterns.
findings.recommendation: Low priority for CLI tool. Consider GitHub Actions CI failure alerts as minimal signal.

findings.title: Unit tests only with mocked AWS interactions
findings.description: Test suite exists with Mocha + Chai + aws-sdk-mock. All AWS interactions mocked. No integration tests against real or simulated AWS services.
findings.gap: All AWS interactions mocked. No integration tests validate actual API call sequences or end-to-end CLI workflows.
findings.recommendation: Add integration test suite using localstack to validate deploy/package workflows against simulated AWS services.

findings.title: No incident response or troubleshooting documentation
findings.description: No runbooks or structured troubleshooting guides. Users receive raw AWS SDK error messages with no guided resolution.
findings.gap: No runbooks for common deployment failures. No structured troubleshooting guide.
findings.recommendation: Add troubleshooting guide documenting common deployment errors with resolution steps.

findings.title: No observability ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS file, no monitoring dashboards, no team-attributed alarms. Open-source project with implicit single-maintainer ownership.
findings.gap: No structured ownership of observability or operational health.
findings.recommendation: Add CODEOWNERS file. Low priority for open-source CLI.

pathways.name: Move to Cloud Native
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: APP-Q2=2 meets primary trigger but all supporting INF conditions are N/A (no deployed infrastructure). CLI tool does not need cloud-native decomposition.

triggering_questions.note: APP-Q2=2 but all INF supporting triggers are N/A; pathway not meaningful for a CLI tool

pathways.not_triggered_reason: While APP-Q2<3, all supporting conditions (INF-Q1, APP-Q3, APP-Q4) are Not Evaluated. This CLI tool is not a deployable service requiring cloud-native decomposition.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Not applicable — this CLI tool has no deployed workload to containerize. has_deployed_workload=false.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No commercial database engines detected. DATA-Q4 is Not Evaluated (no database). Tool already uses open-source dependencies.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines or proprietary SQL detected. The tool itself is open-source.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No databases detected. INF-Q2 is Not Evaluated. has_persistent_data_store=false.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: This CLI tool has no database. has_persistent_data_store=false.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No data processing workloads. INF-Q4 is Not Evaluated. No streaming or ETL artifacts found.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist. Contextual guard blocks: no streaming/ETL/analytics artifacts found.

pathways.name: Move to Modern DevOps
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: OPS-Q6<3 (integration test gaps in CI pipeline). CI exists for testing but no deployment automation or comprehensive integration tests.

triggering_questions.note: Integration tests limited to mocked unit tests; no end-to-end validation

detail.current_state: GitHub Actions CI runs tests on Node 22.x/24.x across Ubuntu/macOS/Windows. CodeQL scanning. Dependabot monthly updates. No automated npm publish. All tests use aws-sdk-mock.

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context.
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No AI/agent intent detected in portfolio or service context. Context mentions 'Node.js CLI for deploying AWS Lambda functions' with no AI-related signal terms.
